description:
You are a newly-appointed compliance officer.  
Your first assignment is to check whether every JSON system-configuration file located in  
    /home/user/data/audit/  
conforms to the minimal corporate schema that has been supplied in  

    /home/user/data/schema/system_config.schema.json

Assume the schema requires that *every* JSON object contains **all four** of the following members with the exact data-types:

1. hostname – string  
2. os_version – string  
3. patch_level – integer (whole number)  
4. encryption_enabled – boolean  

Nothing else needs to be validated; presence and correct primitive type are enough.

Your job is to:

1. Examine every “*.json” file inside /home/user/data/audit/.  
2. Using jq (or any other scripting tool that is pre-installed in the container) decide, per file, whether it satisfies the above schema.  
3. Produce a log file called  

       /home/user/audit_results/compliance.log  

   that summarises the outcome for *each* file **in exactly the format shown below, line-by-line**.  
4. Append a final summary line that counts passes and failures.

Required compliance.log format (must match exactly, character-for-character):

• For every examined file write one line:  
      <filename>::<result>::<errors>  

  – <filename> is the file’s basename (e.g. “server.json”, no directory path).  
  – <result> is either PASS or FAIL (all capitals).  
  – <errors> is a list of the offending keys, **in the order shown in the schema above**, separated by a semicolon “;”.  
    If the file passes validation, write a single hyphen character “-” instead.

• After all per-file lines, write **one additional summary line**:  
      TOTAL_PASS=<number> TOTAL_FAIL=<number>

• Use a single UNIX line-feed (LF, “\n”) after every line, including the last one.

Example of what two lines for one good and one bad file would look like (for illustration only):

    good.json::PASS::-
    bad.json::FAIL::patch_level;encryption_enabled

NO extra text, headers, blank lines or whitespace are allowed anywhere in compliance.log.

Practical constraints:

• Create the directory /home/user/audit_results/ if it does not exist.  
• The script/commands you write must work with standard tools typically available in a basic Linux container (bash, jq, awk, etc.).  
• You do NOT need to modify the original JSON files or the schema file.  
• Do not require root privileges. All work must be done as the regular user.  
• When you are finished, you should be able to display compliance.log and see the correct PASS/FAIL lines followed by the TOTAL_PASS / TOTAL_FAIL summary.

The automated tester will:

1. Verify that /home/user/audit_results/compliance.log exists and is readable.  
2. Check that its contents conform *exactly* to the specification above, including newline placement.  
3. Confirm that the PASS/FAIL counts reflect the actual validation results of the files provided in /home/user/data/audit/.

Perform whatever sequential terminal commands are necessary to achieve this outcome and leave the system in the required final state.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before the agent begins):

/home/user/data/schema/system_config.schema.json  
------------------------------------------------
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["hostname", "os_version", "patch_level", "encryption_enabled"],
  "properties": {
    "hostname":           { "type": "string"  },
    "os_version":         { "type": "string"  },
    "patch_level":        { "type": "integer" },
    "encryption_enabled": { "type": "boolean" }
  },
  "additionalProperties": true
}

/home/user/data/audit/system1.json  
----------------------------------
{
  "hostname": "webserver01",
  "os_version": "Ubuntu 20.04",
  "patch_level": 5,
  "encryption_enabled": true
}

/home/user/data/audit/system2.json  
----------------------------------
{
  "hostname": "dbserver02",
  "os_version": "CentOS 8"
}

/home/user/data/audit/system3.json  
----------------------------------
{
  "hostname": 12345,
  "os_version": "Debian 10",
  "patch_level": 7,
  "encryption_enabled": true
}

Expected final file created by the agent:

/home/user/audit_results/compliance.log  
---------------------------------------
system1.json::PASS::-
system2.json::FAIL::patch_level;encryption_enabled
system3.json::FAIL::hostname
TOTAL_PASS=1 TOTAL_FAIL=2

(Each of the four lines above is terminated with a single LF. No trailing blank line.)

The automated test will parse this file verbatim to verify task completion.
